methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $27M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
LabGenius, a Seed-stage biotech company founded by James Field, has raised $73M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 3 public peers in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-sector peers including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals.

Only one valuation method proved applicable given data constraints. Stage-Progression anchored to a $27M base value derived from total capital raised, applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $68M. A sensitivity range accounting for ±15% variance around this single method supports a low of $57M and a high of $79M.

Public-Comp Anchoring could not be applied; while the Biotech & Health sector trades at a 6.9x P/S median across the three public comps, LabGenius's revenue is not on file, as is standard for private companies. Peer-Median triangulation was similarly inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage private comparables meeting narrow filters. The analysis supports a valuation range of $57M–$79M, centered at $68M, with the primary uncertainty being the applicability of a generalized stage multiplier to this specific asset absent revenue visibility or tighter peer benchmarking.
